key_themes: Early success of 'Back to Starbucks' turnaround with 4% comp sales and transaction growth
Margin compression and EPS declines from labor investments and costs
Mixed analyst actions including Jefferies upgrade to Hold but RBC downgrade on lofty expectations and valuation
Ongoing labor union tensions and bad-faith bargaining claims
High valuation with stock recovering toward $100 amid cautious investor views ahead of Q2 earnings
summary: Starbucks reported solid Q1 FY2026 results with 6% revenue growth to $9.9B and 4% global/US comp store sales led by transactions—the first positive traffic trend in eight quarters—as the 'Back to Starbucks' plan gains traction and is described as ahead of schedule. However, significant margin pressure from labor investments caused a 19% drop in non-GAAP EPS to $0.56 (missing estimates), with full-year guidance calling for 3%+ comps and cautious profitability. Stock has recovered to around $100 but faces mixed analyst sentiment on valuation, union risks, competition, and sustainability of the rebound ahead of Q2 earnings on April 28.
sources: 15+ news articles (Starbucks Investor Relations, Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, CNBC, MarketBeat), ~25 recent X posts (primarily analyst notes, union concerns; high spam volume, low organic investor discussion)
